title: Cleisostoma minax
taxonomy: Cleisostoma minax is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. Cleisostoma minax is specifically placed within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Cleisostoma is part of the tribe Cranichideae. Further classification of Cleisostoma minax also involves its placement within the section and subsection levels.

intro: Cleisostoma minax, also known as the 'dwarf orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions including wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents present in Cleisostoma minax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cleisostoma minax is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are valued for their potential therapeutic benefits and are used to support overall health and well-being.
